channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tornado damages structures and oil sites in Alberta hamlet, officials report
item.description: A tornado struck the east-central Alberta hamlet of Tulliby Lake, causing damage to several structures, oil sites, and local vegetation. County officials from Vermilion River confirmed the event in a ...

item.title: Iran's Lost Literary Voice: Shahrnush Parsipur's Legacy in Persian Literature
item.description: Shahrnush Parsipur, an Iranian feminist writer and novelist, passed away at 80, leaving behind a legacy that transformed modern Persian literature. Over five decades, she challenged societal norms by ...

item.title: No Winners in Major Lotto Draws on July 12, 2026
item.description: On Sunday, July 12, 2026, there were no winners for the major lotto games held by the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PSCO). The Ultra Lotto 6/58 game's jackpot remained at P128,000,000.00 as n...

item.title: Common Causes of Nighttime Urination and How to Manage Them
item.description: Nighttime urination, or nocturia, is a common issue affecting many people's quality of life. Experts suggest that factors such as pregnancy, excessive water intake before bed, and age-related conditio...

item.title: Mitra Samal's Poetry Explores Memory, Desire, and Emotional Intimacy
item.description: Mitra Samal's poetry collection 'Silence Has a Sound' delves into themes of memory, desire, and emotional intimacy through five thematic sections. The poems, often set in domestic spheres, explore per...

item.title: El Niño's Impact on South Africa's Weather and Agriculture
item.description: A strong El Niño climate pattern is developing in the Pacific Ocean, with a 90% chance of persisting until November. This phenomenon is expected to bring extreme heat, drought, and veld fires across S...

item.title: Van der Poel clinches Tour de France stage nine victory as Pogacar extends lead
item.description: Mathieu van der Poel secured his third career Tour de France stage win on Sunday after a dramatic finish to stage nine, which saw him triumph in a chaotic scramble for the line. The 31-year-old Dutch ...

item.title: Tanzania Warns Against Fake Pesticides Threatening Farmers' Livelihoods
item.description: Tanzanian authorities and agricultural experts are alarmed by the spread of counterfeit pesticides in local markets, comparing sellers of fake agricultural inputs to murderers due to their devastating...

item.title: Federal Government Raises WAEC and NECO Exam Fees to ₦50,000 Starting 2027
item.description: The Federal Government has approved a new examination fee of ₦50,000 for WAEC and NECO candidates from 2027, nearly doubling the current rates. This decision has sparked widespread criticism from stud...

item.title: Suspect arrested in Ann Widdecombe murder investigation after suspicious car spotted near her Dartmoor home
item.description: Police have arrested a 28-year-old man from Rotherham in connection with the death of former UK minister Ann Widdecombe, following an investigation into a suspicious vehicle spotted near her Dartmoor ...

item.title: CISA Had No Prepared Response Plan for Cybersecurity Incident, Report Reveals
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) admitted it lacked a pre-existing response plan for handling a cybersecurity incident in May, according to a postmortem report released...

item.title: FBI Clarifies Role in Investigating Lindsey Graham's Death After Director's Controversial Comments
item.description: The FBI faced scrutiny after its director, Kash Patel, made a controversial social media post about the sudden death of Senator Lindsey Graham, sparking accusations of recklessness. Critics, including...

item.title: Manohar Lal Addresses Patronage Impact on CPWD Transfers
item.description: The article discusses Manohar Lal's comments on the persistent issue of patronage influencing transfer processes within the Central Public Works Department (CPWD). Lal highlighted how political connec...

item.title: Newfoundland's Role in Early News Distribution via Telegraph
item.description: The article explores the historical significance of Cape Race, Newfoundland, as a crucial hub for news dissemination in 19th-century North America. Before modern communication technologies, Cape Race ...

item.title: Political Tensions and Youth Frustrations in Tanzania
item.description: The article discusses the political climate in Tanzania following the October 2025 election crisis, highlighting the fear and confusion among citizens as security forces maintain a heavy presence. Maj...

item.title: Right-wing activist Tommy Robinson linked to Russian funding and UK political figures
item.description: British right-wing activist Tommy Robinson has sparked controversy after revelations he travelled to Moscow with support from Elon Musk's family foundation, coinciding with his growing relationship wi...

item.title: Kenya's Junior Starlets Qualify for 2026 FIFA U-17 Women's World Cup
item.description: Kenya's Junior Starlets have secured their place in the 2026 FIFA U-17 Women's World Cup after defeating South Africa 3-1 in the final qualification round. National Economic Development Party leader M...

item.title: NBAA sets up pavilion at Sabasaba to guide youth into accounting careers
item.description: The National Board of Accountants and Auditors (NBAA) is actively engaging with young people at the Dar es Salaam International Trade Fair (Sabasaba) to promote careers in accounting and auditing. The...

item.title: Pokemon Company Considers Price Hike and Encouraging Dual Version Purchases
item.description: An internal document from The Pokemon Company reveals past considerations to increase the price of Pokemon games beyond the original $60 per version during the Nintendo Switch era. The document also h...

item.title: Finance Minister Godongwana redirects funds to Eskom and water boards to address municipal compliance issues
item.description: Finance Minister Enoch Godongwana has endorsed the National Treasury's decision to withhold R13.5bn from 69 non-compliant municipalities, redirecting funds directly to Eskom, water boards, and statuto...
